coreJobStatement: Generate, distribute, and meter thermal energy (heating and cooling) from centralised plants to multiple buildings via closed-pipe networks
hierarchy: Process VN (L7 through L4) — what process steps, equipment, and subsystems compose district energy generation, distribution, and consumption metering
marketSize: ~$187–214B globally (district heating, 2024); heat meter market projected to reach $3.24B by 2035; EU EED mandates individual metering at every building connection [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: CHP Plant (Combined Heat and Power)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Gas turbine or reciprocating engine generating electricity with waste heat captured for district heating. Dominant generation source in European networks. Electrical efficiency 35–45%; total efficiency 80–90%. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Hot-Water Boiler Plant
vnUnits.functionalJob: Large-scale hot-water boilers (gas, oil, biomass, electric) for peak-load and backup generation. Typical supply temperatures 80–120 °C (3rd generation) or 50–70 °C (4th generation low-temperature networks). [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Large-Scale Heat Pump
vnUnits.functionalJob: Electric heat pump extracting thermal energy from ambient sources (seawater, wastewater, ground) for low-temperature district heating. Growing rapidly in Scandinavian networks. COP 3–5 typical. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Chiller Plant (District Cooling)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Centralised electric or absorption chillers producing chilled water (5–7 °C supply) for district cooling networks. Electric chillers hold ~47% market share in district cooling. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Thermal Energy Storage (TES)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Centralised storage buffering generation/demand mismatch. Hot-water tanks (2,000–50,000 m3) for heating; ice or chilled-water tanks for cooling. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Waste Heat Recovery
vnUnits.functionalJob: Captures industrial waste heat (data centres, wastewater treatment, industrial processes) for injection into district heating network. Zero-fuel marginal cost. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Pre-Insulated Pipe Network
vnUnits.functionalJob: Bonded pre-insulated pipe (steel carrier pipe + PU foam insulation + PE jacket) buried underground. Supply and return pipes run in parallel. Key manufacturers: Logstor, Isoplus, Brugg, Permapipe. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Network Pumping Station
vnUnits.functionalJob: Centralised and distributed pumps maintaining hydraulic pressure and flow across the network. Variable-speed drives dominate modern networks for energy-efficient flow control.

vnUnits.name: Network Expansion & Pressure Management
vnUnits.functionalJob: Compensates for thermal expansion of carrier pipes and maintains system pressure within design limits.

vnUnits.name: Network Valves & Fittings
vnUnits.functionalJob: Branch isolation, sectionalising, and flow regulation across the distribution network.

vnUnits.name: Heat Exchanger (Substation)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Plate heat exchanger hydraulically separating primary (network) from secondary (building) circuits. Prevents pressure and water-quality cross-contamination. Brazed or gasketed plate design; 50–2,000 kW typical per building. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Substation Control Valve Assembly
vnUnits.functionalJob: Motorised control valve regulating primary-side flow to maintain secondary supply temperature setpoint. The control valve is the primary actuator; the flow sensor provides feedback for the energy calculation. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Substation Flow & Temperature Measurement
vnUnits.functionalJob: Instruments measuring volumetric flow rate and supply/return temperatures on both primary and secondary sides. The flow signal and paired temperature signals feed the energy calculation unit for billing-grade thermal energy measurement. This is the primary integration point for Marquardt's ultrasonic flow sensor in district energy. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Secondary-Side Distribution
vnUnits.functionalJob: Building-internal hydronic distribution fed by the substation's secondary circuit.

vnUnits.name: Heat Meter / Thermal Energy Meter
vnUnits.functionalJob: MID-certified instrument integrating flow × density × Cp × delta-T over time to calculate delivered kWh or MWh. Ultrasonic meters dominate new installations. Kamstrup MULTICAL, Danfoss SonoMeter, Siemens UH50 are market leaders. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Cooling Meter
vnUnits.functionalJob: Similar to heat meter but measuring extracted cooling energy. Reverse delta-T calculation (return warmer than supply). Growing with district cooling adoption.

vnUnits.name: Meter Data Communication
vnUnits.functionalJob: Remote reading, data collection, and billing system integration. AMR (automatic meter reading) and AMI (advanced metering infrastructure) enable real-time consumption visibility.

vnUnits.name: SCADA & Network Monitoring
vnUnits.functionalJob: Centralised supervisory control and data acquisition system monitoring plant, network, and substations.

vnUnits.name: Hydraulic Balancing & Optimisation
vnUnits.functionalJob: Algorithms and actuators maintaining balanced flow distribution across the network to minimise pump energy and ensure adequate supply to all buildings.

vnUnits.name: Leak Detection & Pipe Integrity
vnUnits.functionalJob: Monitoring buried pipe network for insulation degradation and carrier pipe leaks.

vnUnits.name: Water Treatment & Corrosion Protection
vnUnits.functionalJob: Maintaining water chemistry in the closed network to prevent corrosion, scaling, and biological growth.

vnUnits.name: Regulatory Compliance & Safety
vnUnits.functionalJob: Pressure equipment directive, building safety codes, environmental permits, and metering calibration.
